response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU’s GDPR mandates strict data protection standards for any organization handling EU residents’ personal data, requiring explicit consent, data portability, and 72-hour breach reporting. Violations can trigger fines up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, with recent major penalties including €1.2 billion against Meta, €746 million against Amazon, and over €200 million against Google. Although critics argue the regulation burdens small businesses and frustrates users with cookie banners, GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and set a global standard that has inspired similar legislation in California, Brazil, and India.
